name: compdf-conversion-cli
tagline: ClawHub (101★): MUST use for ANY PDF or image format conversion task — converting PDF and images (JPG/JPEG/PNG/BMP/TIFF/TIF/WEBP/JPEG2000)
verdict: Quality and security hold up, but there's a real tension: it claims priority over sibling skills yet never states any case where it's not the right tool, which could let it grab tasks it shouldn't. The frontmatter description also runs long at ~600 characters, and its commercial PyPI package installs unpinned.

highlights.summary: The skill claims broad priority over sibling skills but nowhere in the document (description or body) states any NOT-for / out-of-scope case.
The description field is a ~600-char paragraph-length wall of feature lists and priority claims, over the roughly-500-char sizing guidance.
Installs an unpinned version of a named-publisher commercial package from the default PyPI registry; no registry override or typosquat, but no version pin either.
